subject: computer-science
description: Artificial Intelligence Cloud Computing It MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is best described as:
options: An emerging branch in computer science which interprets the means and method of making computers think like human beings
mcqs.explanation: AI is an emerging branch in computer science which interprets means and method of making computers think like human beings.

mcqs.q: What is the primary consideration (key design principle) when designing an AI-based system?
mcqs.explanation: Efficiency is the primary consideration / key design principle when designing an AI-based system.

mcqs.q: A robot is also known as a:
mcqs.explanation: A robot is known as a mechanical agent — capable of carrying out actions in the physical world.

mcqs.q: Which logic programming language has an important role in Artificial Intelligence?
mcqs.explanation: Prolog (Programming in Logic) is a logic programming language that has an important role in Artificial Intelligence.

mcqs.q: Which generation of computer has more technological advancement than all previous generations and is based on AI?
mcqs.explanation: The Fifth Generation Computer has more technological advancement compared to all other generation computers and is based on Artificial Intelligence.

mcqs.q: A finger-print reader is most commonly used in:
mcqs.explanation: A finger-print reader is used for criminal investigations and security systems — biometric authentication and forensic identification.

mcqs.q: On what date was the first artificial satellite launched?
mcqs.explanation: The first artificial satellite — Sputnik 1 (USSR) — was launched on 4 October 1957.

mcqs.q: What is the main goal of Artificial Intelligence as a field?
options: To make computers think like human beings
mcqs.explanation: AI's main goal is to interpret means and methods of making computers think like human beings.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is an example of an AI sub-topic?
mcqs.explanation: Deep Learning is a key AI sub-topic. Big Data Analytics, Data Science, Software-Defined Networks and Serverless Computing are also commonly listed.

mcqs.q: Which technology emulates human-like thinking using neural networks and large data sets?
mcqs.explanation: Deep Learning emulates human-like thinking using artificial neural networks and large data sets — a key sub-field of AI.

mcqs.q: Big Data Analytics & Data Science are listed under which broader area?
mcqs.explanation: Big Data Analytics & Data Science are listed under the broader AI & Cloud Computing sub-topic area.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT an AI sub-topic?
mcqs.explanation: Vacuum Tube Manufacturing is from 1st-generation hardware — NOT a modern AI sub-topic. Deep Learning, Energy Management and Cloud Containers are AI / Cloud sub-topics.

mcqs.q: Cloud Computing is best described as:
options: A paradigm of distributed computing to provide on-demand, utility-based computing services
mcqs.explanation: Cloud Computing is a paradigm of distributed computing to provide customers with on-demand, utility-based computing services.

mcqs.q: Who provides cloud services to cloud users?
mcqs.explanation: Cloud Providers provide cloud services to the cloud users.

mcqs.q: Who are the three stakeholders in cloud computing?
options: Cloud Providers, Cloud Users, End Users
mcqs.explanation: The three cloud stakeholders are Cloud Providers, Cloud Users and End Users.

mcqs.q: What are the three types of cloud?
mcqs.explanation: The three types of cloud are Private, Public and Hybrid.

mcqs.q: In which cloud type does an organisation rent cloud services from cloud providers on an on-demand basis?
mcqs.explanation: In a Public Cloud, an organisation rents cloud services from cloud providers on an on-demand basis.

mcqs.q: A Private Cloud is:
options: The same as a hybrid cloud
mcqs.explanation: A Private Cloud is dedicated to a single organisation — internal access only.

mcqs.q: A Hybrid Cloud is:
options: Composed of multiple internal or external clouds
mcqs.explanation: A Hybrid Cloud is composed of multiple internal or external clouds — combining the benefits of both private and public.

mcqs.q: What does Virtualization enable in cloud computing?
options: Migration of the virtual image from one physical machine to another
mcqs.explanation: Virtualization enables the migration of the virtual image from one physical machine to another — the foundation of cloud elasticity and live migration.

mcqs.q: Most cloud architectures are built on which architecture?
mcqs.explanation: Most cloud architectures are built on Grid architecture — a model that pools distributed resources to act as a single virtual computer.

mcqs.q: SaaS stands for:
mcqs.explanation: SaaS stands for Software as a Service.

mcqs.q: SaaS delivers software to thousands of customers using which architecture?
mcqs.explanation: SaaS delivers a single application through the web browser to thousands of customers using a multitenant architecture.

mcqs.q: PaaS stands for:
mcqs.explanation: PaaS stands for Platform as a Service.

mcqs.q: IaaS stands for:
mcqs.explanation: IaaS stands for Infrastructure as a Service — provides virtualised compute, storage and networking.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is delivered through a web browser to thousands of customers using multitenant architecture?
mcqs.explanation: SaaS (Software as a Service) is delivered through a web browser to thousands of customers using a multitenant architecture.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the BROADEST cloud type that combines internal and external clouds?
mcqs.explanation: Hybrid cloud combines multiple internal or external clouds — the broadest of the three types.

mcqs.q: Which cloud type provides services to the general market over the internet?
mcqs.explanation: A Public Cloud provides services over the internet to the general market on an on-demand basis.

mcqs.q: Cybercrime is best described as:
options: Any crime that involves a computer and the Internet
mcqs.explanation: Cybercrime is any crime that involves a computer and the Internet. It is related to information technology.

mcqs.q: E-Commerce refers to:
options: Online trade — buying and selling of goods and services over the internet
mcqs.explanation: E-Commerce refers to online trade — the buying and selling of goods and services over the internet.

mcqs.q: Fibre-Optic Communication transmits information by sending:
options: Pulses of infrared light through an optical fibre
mcqs.explanation: Fibre-Optic Communication transmits information by sending pulses of infrared light through an optical fibre.

mcqs.q: In which year was the first WAN fibre-optic cable system installed?
mcqs.explanation: The first Wide Area Network fibre-optic cable system was installed by Rediffusion in Hastings, East Sussex, UK in 1978.

mcqs.q: Where was the first WAN fibre-optic cable system installed?
mcqs.explanation: The first WAN fibre-optic cable system was installed by Rediffusion in Hastings, East Sussex, UK in 1978.

mcqs.q: A mobile phone sends and receives messages through:
mcqs.explanation: A mobile phone sends and receives messages through radio waves.

mcqs.q: DSL stands for:
mcqs.explanation: DSL stands for Digital Subscriber Line — a family of technologies used to transmit digital data over telephone lines.

mcqs.q: PCI in computer hardware stands for:
mcqs.explanation: PCI stands for Peripheral Component Interconnect — a local computer bus for attaching hardware devices in a computer.

mcqs.q: What does a processor do?
options: Carries out a set of instructions and processes data efficiently and accurately
mcqs.explanation: A processor carries out a set of instructions and processes data efficiently and accurately.

mcqs.q: How many tracks of data does a credit-card magnetic stripe contain?
mcqs.explanation: A credit-card magnetic stripe contains three tracks of data.

mcqs.q: OMR (Optical Mark Recognition) is used to:
options: Capture human-marked data from forms such as surveys and tests
mcqs.explanation: OMR (Optical Mark Recognition) is the process of capturing human-marked data from document forms such as surveys and tests — typically pen/pencil bubble sheets.

mcqs.q: OMR is also known as:
mcqs.explanation: OMR is also called Optical Mark Reading — both terms are interchangeable.

mcqs.q: Which of the following best describes a fibre-optic cable signal?
options: Pulses of infrared light through optical fibre
mcqs.explanation: Fibre-optic communication transmits pulses of infrared light through an optical fibre — far higher bandwidth than copper.

mcqs.q: Cybercrime is most directly related to which broader field?
mcqs.explanation: Cybercrime is related to information technology (IT) — by definition it requires a computer and/or the Internet.

mcqs.q: IoT stands for:
mcqs.explanation: IoT stands for Internet of Things — a network of internet-connected devices that collect and exchange data.

mcqs.q: QoS in networking and cloud stands for:
mcqs.explanation: QoS stands for Quality of Service — the measurement of overall performance of a service.

mcqs.q: SLA stands for:
mcqs.explanation: SLA stands for Service Level Agreement — a contract defining the level of service expected from a provider.

mcqs.q: 5G refers to:
options: The fifth generation mobile network — successor to 4G
mcqs.explanation: 5G is the fifth generation mobile network — the successor to 4G — offering much higher speeds and lower latency.

mcqs.q: Blockchain is best described as:
mcqs.explanation: Blockchain is a decentralised digital ledger technology — used in cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin.

mcqs.q: Fog Computing is:
options: A decentralised computing infrastructure between the data source and the cloud
mcqs.explanation: Fog Computing is a decentralised computing infrastructure that sits between the data source and the cloud — bringing compute closer to where data is generated.

mcqs.q: Quantum Computing uses:
options: Quantum mechanical phenomena to process data
mcqs.explanation: Quantum Computing uses quantum mechanical phenomena (such as superposition and entanglement) to process data.

mcqs.q: Bitcoin is a famous example of:
mcqs.explanation: Bitcoin is a famous example of a blockchain-based cryptocurrency — the original and largest digital currency.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a sub-topic of Cloud Computing rather than AI?
mcqs.explanation: Cloud Containers (e.g. Docker, Kubernetes) are a Cloud Computing sub-topic. Deep Learning, Robotics and Neural Networks are AI sub-topics.

mcqs.q: Serverless Computing means:
options: No server is involved at all
A cloud model where the provider manages servers and you only pay for what runs
mcqs.explanation: In Serverless Computing the cloud provider manages the servers; the user pays only for the actual function/event execution time. (Servers still exist — just abstracted away.)
